body.title: [sprint] Replace dead /autocompact cadence with native /compact fired at ~150K context tokens
body.intention: I intend that a CC session compacts (via native /compact) when its live context reaches ~150K tokens — so long sessions stay fresh (avoid the 'Opus felt like Haiku' context-rot) without relying on the harness's late context-%% native trigger, and without the retired /autocompact machinery.
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-03: 'let's try 150K. and we can now remove autocompact.' The old ticket-close cadence hook (cc_compact_cadence.py) is dead three ways: (1) settings.json registered it as `python3 -m devices.claude.cc_compact_cadence` but post single-package-reorg the module is `unseen_university.devices.claude.cc_compact_cadence` — import fails, silently no-ops (`|| true`); (2) baseline desynced (102 vs current 2); (3) it only counts /sprint-ticket closes via sprint_token_log.py, so manual cc_queue.py closes never increment it. It also fires the RETIRED /autocompact (native /compact is canonical per feedback_native_compact_no_autocompact). There is NO native Claude Code setting for a numeric token compaction threshold — native auto-compact is context-%%-based (fired ~300K this session). So 150K must be implemented as a hook.

The settings.json Stop-hook REGISTRATION was already removed 2026-07-03 (backup ~/.claude/settings.json.bak-20260703). This ticket does the rest.

**What to build:**
1. New Stop hook (replace cc_compact_cadence): reads the Stop-hook stdin payload's `transcript_path`, sums the session's context tokens (or reads the harness-provided usage), and when live context >= CC_COMPACT_AT_TOKENS (default 150000) injects native `/compact` (NOT /autocompact) into the CC tmux session — same tmux send-keys pattern, command `/compact`. Guard against re-fire (the compaction turn itself must not re-trigger: after firing, record a baseline / cooldown so it doesn't loop).
2. DELETE the dead machinery: cc_compact_cadence.py, its COMPACT_EVERY_N / compact_baseline_path / sprint_tokens_log_path constants IF unused elsewhere (grep first), and the ClaudeShim.start() registration that adds the Stop hook (so it isn't re-added on shim start).
3. Register the new hook (via ClaudeShim.start() or settings.json — match the existing pattern).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/claude/cc_compact_cadence.py (delete/replace), unseen_university/devices/claude/constants.py (COMPACT_EVERY_N etc.), unseen_university/devices/claude/shim.py (ClaudeShim.start hook registration), ~/.claude/settings.json (new hook registration), tests/ (new proof test)
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not; no-sqlite (n/a); external-state (baseline/cooldown on disk, shim-owned)
**Scope boundary:** IN: token-threshold Stop hook firing native /compact + full removal of dead ticket-close cadence machinery + re-fire guard. OUT: changing the native harness auto-compact behavior (can't); tuning the threshold live (env var CC_COMPACT_AT_TOKENS is enough).
**Test plan (real red->green):** unit test the decision fn should_compact_by_tokens(current_tokens, threshold, cooldown_state) -> bool: returns True at >=150000 when not in cooldown, False below, False when in cooldown (re-fire guard). Stub: guard/threshold logic absent -> re-fires or never fires -> RED; impl -> GREEN. Plus a test that the injected command string is `/compact`, not `/autocompact`.
**Validation (runtime):** a long session posts a `/compact` (visible in tmux + the session compacts) when context crosses ~150K, and does NOT re-fire on the post-compaction turn.
**Logging:** log.info('cc-compact: context=%d >= %d, firing native /compact') at fire; log.debug for below-threshold ticks.
**Observable via:** log.info('cc-compact: firing native /compact') + the /compact appearing in the CC tmux pane.
**Rollback:** git revert restores prior (dead) hook; the settings.json registration is already removed so no compaction hook runs until this ships.
**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (re-fire guard is the point; a trigger that loops on its own compaction turn is the failure mode).
